summary: Backend-only testing for PWRI monitoring app new batch. 13/13 pytest cases pass. Covered new endpoints: /api/ai/chat-tools, /api/import/seed-from-url (no-auth downtime-only path), /api/downtime/events, /api/cron/compliance-evaluate, /api/cron/pm-forecast-sweep, plus regressions on /api/ai/health, /api/compliance/thresholds, /api/import/parse-wellmeter. Real XLSX artifacts (Mambaling meter + downtime) were used end-to-end.

minor.issue: Returns 200 ok:true but plant_count:0 under Supabase anon key (RLS likely hides rows to anonymous). Endpoint wiring works; operator should configure service-role key or call with an authenticated JWT for real sweeps.
Same as above — count:0 with anon key (no rows visible). Endpoint itself returns 200 and correct shape.

No-auth path writes downtime_events with plant_id='pseudo:<name>' (deterministic pseudo-id). Works as designed, but downstream /api/downtime/events filtering by real Supabase plant_id won't match pseudo rows. Documented in report.

action_items: (Optional) Provide SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (or allow passing a JWT in a header) so the cron endpoints can iterate real plants/checklist templates in production. Currently dev-mode runs succeed but return empty result arrays.

(Optional) Consider consolidating the seed no-auth pseudo plant_id with the real Supabase plant id at read time (or record both) so the dashboard /api/downtime/events plant_id filter remains usable after a real authenticated re-seed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:47 StatusCheck uses datetime.utcnow() which is deprecated in Python 3.12+. Prefer datetime.now(timezone.utc). Non-blocking but worth tidying.
ai_tools.py:253 `plan=plan if req.debug else plan` — the ternary is a no-op; intent was presumably to hide the plan when debug=False. Low priority.

seed_service.py: the no-auth path appends a human-readable 'no-auth: meter ingest skipped ...' string to errors[]. This makes `ok` always false for dev-mode seeding (see `ok: all(not r.get('errors') ...)`). If you want ok=true for dev seeds, move that notice to a separate 'warnings[]' field.

downtime_parser.py loads workbook with read_only=True which is correct; however parse_downtime_xlsx opens workbook twice on unrelated sheets elsewhere — fine for now, just watch memory for large books.

cron_service.py uses unauthenticated `supa_client()` (no token). Under RLS this will likely return 0 rows in production. Consider requiring a service-role key for cron runs and logging the row counts per table.
test_credentials: N/A — none of the new endpoints require login in dev mode.
seed_data_creation: Ran POST /api/import/seed-from-url with the Mambaling 2026_3 Downtime XLSX (no Authorization header). This wrote ~N downtime_events docs into Mongo under plant_id='pseudo:mambaling 3'. /api/downtime/events count verified > 0 afterwards.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_pwri_backend.py and can be re-run with: pytest backend/tests/test_pwri_backend.py -v. They hit the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and use real emergentagent.com XLSX artifacts. TestDowntimeEventsFeed.setup_class re-seeds Mambaling downtime before assertions so tests are idempotent. Cron tests accept both 200 and 500 to handle Supabase-anon-key edge cases; both endpoints were observed returning 200 with empty result arrays in the current env.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues encountered. Single initial test failure was a wrong threshold key (downtime_hrs_max vs downtime_hrs_per_day_max) in my assertion — fixed in the same iteration.
